title: Salk Institute
description: Twin laboratory wings framing a courtyard axis open to the Pacific.
body: Kahn's twin laboratory wings are spanned by storey-deep Vierendeel trusses, giving each laboratory a column-free floor plate and a full interstitial service floor above it — infrastructure treated as architecture.

Between the wings, a travertine courtyard is cut by a single water channel aimed at the Pacific horizon; the studies' teak and board-marked concrete have weathered into the material palette of the site.
